rows.notes: Clear centered page title at top. Legible handwriting and no amounts on this line.
Numerals in all three monetary columns are crisp and well aligned to ruling lines: reads 60 pounds, 0s, 0d.
Clear reading of '87' in pounds column, '06' in shillings column and '0' in pence column; alignment with vertical lines unambiguous.
Pounds reading '40' and following zeros are clearly written; the description has an uncertain letter sequence (transcribed as seen: 'Minhindert'), but the monetary columns align cleanly.
Amounts read clearly as 58.0.0; description legible enough to read the place name and 'pro anno'.

Pound and shilling columns align to read '4' and '11' respectively; pence appears to be '6' (written as '06') but the final digit is slightly faded; description text 'Merton Rectory pro Mir.' is partially abbreviated and faint.

Pounds reading '19' and shillings '06' are reasonably clear; the pence digit looks like '8' though slightly cramped against the right margin. Description has abbreviation 'dimid.' which matches the line text.
Monetary columns read as 20.4.6 but the place name is short and the handwriting (Thrupp) is somewhat stylized; digits are sufficiently clear though the pence is slightly compressed.
Small amount '0' pounds, '02' shillings, '06' pence reads clearly. Description reads 'Ralph Marshals Capons' (orthography matches the script).

rows.description: Calot - pt of Annuit. - 1.7.9 qd? & Mic. 2.9.6d
rows.notes: Description line contains multiple abbreviations and small marginal notations; monetary columns align to read '03' pounds, '17' shillings, '09' pence, but the pence digit is slightly ambiguous due to ink overlap with description notes.
Pounds '03' and shillings '05' visible though a faint stroke slightly touches the shillings digit; pence shows as '0'. Description is legible.
Monetary columns read '03' and '05' but the pence column is faint/blank; description contains abbreviation and slightly cramped script making exact wording moderately uncertain.
Clear '01' pounds and '04' shillings; pence shows as '0'. Description 'Hughes pro biennio' readable though ink is slightly lighter than above lines.
Monetary columns read '04.00.00'; the surname is somewhat ambiguous (transcribed as 'Gredway' matching the visible letters), but amounts align well.
Short name 'Franklin' clearly written. Amount shows plainly as 01.00.00 and is well aligned to the ruling lines.
Amounts read as 03 pounds, 10 shillings, 00 pence; digits are clear though the 'y' tail in 'Mynne' slightly intrudes on the space.
Shillings column clearly '10'; pounds are '0' and pence '0'. Description is legible as 'Leaden-Hall'.
Pounds read '06', shillings '17' and pence '05' — all within valid ranges. The shillings numeral '17' is somewhat compressed but legible; place name spelling is slightly uncertain but readable.
Columns align to '05.14.07'; ink for the pence has a minor smudge making the last digit slightly less crisp but still interpretable as '7'.

Pounds '01', shillings '19' and pence '07' visible; shillings are high but valid (under 20). Description contains standard abbreviations; some ink bleed near the shillings digit increases uncertainty slightly.
Amount reads '06.06.08' though the pence digit is lightly penned; description is legible as 'Clifton-Ferry'.
Columns show '05.12.08'. The first two columns are clear; the pence is slightly faint but consistent with the digit shape '8'.

Place name is partially obscured by a small stain and the handwriting is ambiguous (transcribed as 'Broxtheath'); monetary columns appear to read '09.08.08' but the stain crosses the middle of the line so digits could be misread.
The long description is slightly cramped but readable; amounts align to '03.12.07' and are relatively clear.
Pounds '15' is large and distinct, with '00.00' following. The place name contains uncertain letters (transcribed as seen) and the abbreviation 'dim.' is present; overall amounts are clear.
Amount clearly '04.00.00'. The description contains crossed-out or overwritten words (two period names written), but 'Morton Vicarage' is legible and the monetary columns are unambiguous.
Description contains abbreviation and a small contraction; monetary columns show '02.13.04' though the pence digit is slightly faint. Chosen reading based on alignment with ruling lines.
This final line shows a name/label ('Wander.' or 'Warder.') with an 'x' mark in the description area and no monetary figures in the columns; the absence of amounts is clear.
